label: Guide users through a service
description: Help users navigate a service journey that involves multiple functional patterns. Make progress visible and support smooth transitions between functions.
use_when: A service includes more than one functional pattern.
Users may need to complete tasks in a specific order.
Users may pause and return to the service later.
The service needs clear review or completion points.
do_not_use_when: The service consists of a single, simple interaction.
No progression, review, or re-entry is required.

steps.label: Before you start (if function is complex)
Execute function
steps.description: Route user into the relevant functional pattern (authenticate, pay, check eligibility, etc.).

condition.label: Return to task list after function

ux_considerations.text: Avoid duplicating orientation at both service and function levels unless needed.
Use structure, not hand-off screens, to connect functions.
Support non-linear progress and return for all multi-function services.

technical_notes.text: Task completion state must be persisted to support pause and return across sessions.
